turns.text: Here's a Hubble image of a star cluster. Can you describe what you see?
turns.image_description: Hubble image of a star cluster with evenly spaced stars, blue-white coloration, even lighting

turns.text: I observe a densely packed cluster of stars with uniform spacing. The blue-white coloration suggests young, hot stars, and the lighting appears consistent across the cluster.
Now take a look at this enhanced version of the same cluster. What stands out to you?
turns.image_description: Enhanced image showing one star missing, new red giant visible, slight color shift

turns.text: I notice a gap where one star appears to be missing, and there's now a prominent red giant star visible. The overall color has shifted slightly toward warmer tones.

What is the final configuration of the star cluster?

C) Cluster with two new stars and altered shape
A) Cluster with one missing star
B) Cluster with no visible changes
D) Cluster with a newly formed blue star

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 establishes the initial uniform cluster. Turn 2 shows star loss and a red giant formation. Turn 3 reveals two new stars and a permanently altered shape, confirming the evolutionary changes.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Evolutionary changes require tracking over time to identify new formations
